germany.note: (auto) Germany's 18-year-old midfielder will miss the World Cup after suffering a thigh injury in training.

netherlands.note: (auto) Jurrien TimberDefender Netherlands Groin strainGroin strain Injured Not for World Cup 2026 Jun 23, 2026

ghana.note: (auto) This is a major blow for Ghana, who look to Kudus for moments of inspiration.

austria.note: (auto) Christoph Baumgartner Austria Pre-tournament injury Out (not replaced)

england.note: (auto) The star winger has returned to action after playing through the injury late in the club season, but England is likely to monitor his minutes closely during the tournamen

uruguay.note: (auto) Jose Gimenez Uruguay Ankle Nearing return, Uruguay open June 21

mexico.note: (auto) Edson Alvarez Mexico Fitness concern Being monitored

egypt.note: (auto) Mohamed Salah (Egypt): Recovered from a hamstring tear and captains Egypt, who open against Belgium on June 15.

france.note: Saliba playing through a persistent back issue (surgery deferred to after the tournament); Dembele back in contention.

usa.note: (auto) Johnny Cardoso United States Ankle injury

paraguay.note: (auto) Julio Enciso avoided a serious thigh injury, but Paraguay is unlikely to rush its star attacker back into action.

morocco.note: (auto) Morocco could be without one of its top attacking weapons after Ezzalzouli picked up a knee injury in the team's final tune-up match before the World Cup.

argentina.note: Messi (hamstring) and Alvarez (ankle) carrying knocks; Martinez set to start with a fractured finger. All cleared but managed.

croatia.note: (auto) Luka Modric (Croatia): Recovered from a facial fracture and captains Croatia, now at AC Milan, at his fifth and likely final World Cup.

spain.note: (auto) Fermín Lopez: Spain loses a rising midfield star after López was ruled out with a fractured metatarsal, reducing the squad's depth heading into the tournament.

algeria.note: (auto) Luca Zidane (Algeria): Recovered from a fractured jaw and chin, returned wearing a protective mask, and is Algeria’s first-choice goalkeeper for their opener against Arge

turkey.note: (auto) Arda Guler (Turkey): Fully fit and the focal point of Turkey’s midfield on their return to the World Cup for the first time since 2002.

brazil.note: (auto) Brazil's national team doctor said May 28 that the Santos forward will be sidelined for up to three weeks.

canada.note: (auto) Davies has not played for Canada since March 2025, when he tore an ACL in the CONCACAF Nations League finals, and injured a hamstring last month while playing for Bayern

norway.note: Haaland fit and scoring (4-1 v Iraq); captain Odegaard cleared after an injury-hit club season.

senegal.note: Camp reportedly unsettled by off-field issues clouding the campaign; squad talent intact (Mane, Koulibaly, Mendy).
